reasoning:

This caused the stock to rally near $80 before seeing some short-term profit-taking and easing back into the $77 range by Dec 19. 

The statistical time-series linear model estimates a 52% chance that the stock will be higher over the very short term, and the fundamental picture supports an upward rebound from the recent dip. Furthermore, the final weeks of December often see a structural "Santa Claus rally." Therefore, I assign a slightly elevated probability (>0.5) for the one-week forecast. 

As the forecasting horizon stretches out to 1 month and 3 months, variance increases and predicting individual stock performance versus market drift becomes inherently more uncertain, drawing estimates naturally closer to 0.5. Given specific prompt instructions to increase uncertainty (push probabilities closer to 0.5) for more distant horizons, I've laddered the estimates progressively downward from the short-term forecast.
